summaryText: In the second week of the series 'The Road to the Cross,' Pastor Travis delivered a challenging message on the true cost of discipleship. He began by revisiting the call of Jesus from Luke 9:23, emphasizing that to be a disciple requires denying oneself, taking up one's cross daily, and following Him. The pastor clarified that this is not a casual invitation but a call to surrender, moving beyond mere belief or lip service into active obedience. He argued that true faith is demonstrated through obedience, even when it contradicts our feelings or preferences, such as in the difficult act of forgiveness. The central theme was that discipleship involves a death to our old selves—our sinful nature, attitudes, and appetites—so that we can be made new in Christ. This process is not about creating a better version of ourselves but about becoming a completely new creation, as described in 2 Corinthians 5:17. Pastor Travis connected this spiritual death and resurrection to the public declaration of water baptism, explaining its biblical significance as a symbol of burying the old life and rising to new life in Jesus. He passionately invited those who had never been baptized or who felt a need to be baptized again to take this step, especially on the upcoming Good Friday. The message concluded with a call for commitment, urging listeners to move from being casual, 'lukewarm' Christians to becoming true disciples who are willing to die to themselves and live fully for Christ, contributing to the spiritual revival they believe is happening today.
mainPoints: True discipleship requires denying oneself, taking up one's cross daily, and following Jesus, which is an act of surrender and obedience, not just belief.
The Christian life is about dying to our old sinful nature and becoming a new creation in Christ, which is symbolized through the act of water baptism.
There is a need to move from a casual, lukewarm faith to a committed discipleship that is willing to sacrifice and live fully for God, contributing to spiritual revival.

versesMentioned.text: Then he said to them all: 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.'
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!
As they traveled along the road, they came to some water and the eunuch said, 'Look, here is water. What can stand in the way of my being baptized?'
You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives.

Monday.scripture: Luke 9:23 - 'Then he said to them all: "Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me."'
Monday.action: Identify one personal preference or plan you are holding onto and prayerfully lay it down before God.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to deny myself today and take the first step of surrender to Your will.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!'
Tuesday.action: Write down one 'old' attitude or habit you need to bury. Symbolically tear up the paper as an act of release.
Tuesday.prayer: God, thank you for making me new. Help me to live today in the reality of my new identity in Christ.

Wednesday.scripture: Acts 8:36 - '...What can stand in the way of my being baptized?'
Wednesday.action: If you have not been baptized since making a decision for Christ, take a step today by looking up information about baptism at your church or with a trusted believer.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, give me courage to make a public declaration of my faith. Remove any hesitation standing in my way.

Thursday.scripture: Genesis 50:20 - 'You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives.'
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past hurt or difficulty. Write one way God could use it for good or has already used it to shape you.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I trust that You can turn my past pain for good. Help me to see Your redemptive hand at work.

Friday.action: Choose one specific command from Jesus (e.g., to forgive someone, to serve, to give) and obey it today, no matter how you feel.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, empower me to move from agreement to action. I choose to follow You in obedience today.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 - 'The old has gone, the new is here!'
Saturday.action: Do one thing today that reflects your new life in Christ (e.g., encourage someone, avoid a former temptation, spend time in worship).
Saturday.prayer: God, as I prepare for worship tomorrow, remind me that I am a new creation. Let my life today reflect the change You have made.
